evals.prompt: hey, I've been working on this nest-forge package for a while. Can you check if it's ready to publish to npm? I want to make sure I'm not missing anything important before I put it out there.
evals.expected_output: A structured audit report covering all 12 checklist sections with pass/fail status, specific findings for each section, and actionable fix suggestions. Should run CLI commands like npm pack --dry-run, npm audit, npm run build, npm test.
expectations: The output includes a summary table with pass/fail for each audit section
The audit checks package.json fields (name, version, description, exports, files, engines, etc.)
The audit checks TypeScript configuration (strict mode, declarations, build config)
The audit verifies CI/CD workflows exist and are properly configured
The audit checks for NestJS-specific patterns (module exports, forRoot/forRootAsync)
The audit runs at least some CLI commands (npm pack --dry-run, npm audit, or npm run build)
The audit checks documentation completeness (README, LICENSE, CONTRIBUTING, CHANGELOG)
The output provides specific actionable items for any failures found
The audit references or follows the pre-publish checklist from the skill

evals.prompt: I need you to look at my package's API from the perspective of someone who just discovered it on npm. Is it user-friendly? Am I missing anything that NestJS developers would expect from an SDK generator package?
evals.expected_output: A consumer-perspective API review covering import experience, module configuration, service methods, type safety, error handling, and comparison with similar packages. Findings organized by severity.
expectations: The review examines the barrel file (index.ts) exports from a consumer perspective
The review traces a typical usage flow (import, configure, use)
The review checks if all necessary types and interfaces are exported
The review evaluates naming clarity of public methods and classes
The review identifies missing features or sharp edges a consumer would notice
The review organizes findings by severity (blocking, should-fix, nice-to-have)
The review considers peer dependency reasonableness
The review searches online or compares with similar NestJS packages

evals.prompt: I want to release a new version of my package. I added some new helper functions and fixed a bug in the route parameter extraction since v1.0.0. Walk me through the full release process.
evals.expected_output: A step-by-step release walkthrough covering semver decision, pre-release checks, CHANGELOG update, version bump, git tag, and publish verification. Should determine this is a minor bump (new features + bug fix).
expectations: The output recommends the correct semver bump type (minor, since new features were added)
The output includes running quality gates (lint, test, build) before releasing
The output includes updating CHANGELOG.md with the new version entry
The output includes the npm version or manual version bump command
The output includes git tag creation and push commands
The output explains how the release workflow will publish automatically (or provides manual publish steps)
The output includes post-release verification steps (npm view, test install)
